summary: The checkout has zero commits ahead of origin/main, so assessment used current main/trunk state. Existing behavior preserves the test-frontend job ID, runs both browser engines locally by default, and keeps generated-contract verification separate. The requested impact-aware selector outputs, parallel conditional static/browser jobs, engine parameterization, pinned Playwright environment, pure aggregator behavior, artifact removal, and associated coverage are absent; new code work is required.

requirements.description: Impact-aware selector outputs and policies for frontend_static, frontend_browser_chromium, frontend_browser_firefox, and full_frontend, including fail-open and implication invariants, are absent from tools/select_test_suites.py.

A selector-controlled frontend-static job using shallow checkout, npm cache, npm ci --no-audit --no-fund, and npm run frontend:ci is absent; the existing test-frontend job runs the static command unconditionally.
A selector-controlled, fail-fast:false browser-engine matrix that runs Chromium ordinarily and Chromium plus Firefox for full/browser-sensitive cases is absent.
frontend/vitest.browser.config.ts still hardcodes Chromium and Firefox and does not support MOONMIND_BROWSER_ENGINES or another environment-driven engine selector.
Local npm run ui:test:browser continues to run Chromium and Firefox by default through the two hardcoded Vitest instances.

The workflow still runs npx playwright install --with-deps chromium firefox; Playwright is not an explicit exact root dev dependency, no digest-pinned matching environment is used, and no version-alignment contract is present.
The stable test-frontend job ID still exists, preserving the required-check context identity.

test-frontend is not a tiny if:always() pure aggregator: it has no needs dependency set and performs checkout, Node setup, dependency installation, repository commands, and browser installation. It therefore does not provide intentional-skip or selected-result/engine summaries.
Generated-contract detection and the check-generated-contracts result remain separate from test-frontend.
Selector and workflow contract tests for frontend outputs, fail-open behavior, matrix selection, pinned Playwright alignment, pure aggregation, and artifact removal are absent.
The requested frontend CI documentation updates are absent; current TypeScript documentation still describes a single frontend:ci run and possible dashboard-dist upload.
